post.summary: Requests the cancellation of an account.
post.description:
Requests the cancellation of a specific account.

To send this request, you must provide an account ID in the request path.

If an account ID is provided in an identifiable form, it must be encrypted or tokenized.

The operation verifies that there are no incidents, blocks, or related product contracts that could prevent the account closure. If the verification is successful and the value of 'isSettlementRequired' is true in the request body, a settlement is also requested.

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description:
Account ID to be used in the query.

If an account ID is provided in an identifiable form, it must be encrypted or tokenized.

requestBody.description: Data structure containing the request details for requesting the cancellation of an account

post.summary: Confirms that the settlement for a specific cancelled account has been posted.
post.description:
Confirms that the settlement for a specific cancelled account has been posted and, if it has, retrieves the posted settlement details.

To send this request, you must provide an account ID in the request path.

If an account ID is provided in an identifiable form, it must be encrypted or tokenized.

For a posted settlement, the response includes the settlement amount and the resulting balance.

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description:
Account ID to be used in the query.

If an account ID is provided in an identifiable form, it must be encrypted or tokenized.

post.summary: Get a list of incidents that occur when an account cancellation is attempted.
post.description:
Get a list of incidents that occur when the cancellation of a specific account is attempted. This operation is used after the 'POST /{account_id}/request_cancellation' operation returns an error, indicating that there are one or more incidents preventing the account closure. Incidents can be blocks or warnings.

To send this request, you must provide an account ID in the request path. If an account ID is provided in an identifiable form, it must be encrypted or tokenized.

The response includes the list of incidents that caused the 'POST /{account_id}/request_cancellation' operation to return an error and have not yet been resolved.

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: Account ID to be used in the query. If an account ID is provided in an identifiable form, it must be encrypted or tokenized.

post.description:
Cancels a specific account. This operation is used after the 'POST /{account_id}/request_cancellation' operation is successful, confirming that there are no incidents blocking the cancellation.

To send this request, you must provide an account ID in the request path. If an account ID is provided in an identifiable form, it must be encrypted or tokenized.

Account cancellation details can be provided in the request body.

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: Account ID to be used in the query. If an account ID is provided in an identifiable form, it must be encrypted or tokenized.

requestBody.description: Data structure containing the request details for cancelling an accountt

get.summary: Retrieves a list of warnings and blocks for an account.
get.description:
Retrieves a list of warnings and blocks for a specific account.

To send this request, you must provide an account ID in the request path.

If the account ID is provided in an identifiable form, it must be encrypted or tokenized.

The warnings and blocks included in the response can be filtered by first level code, second level code, third level code, status code, action type code, and validity period.

If no query parameters are provided, all warnings and blocks associated with the account are retrieved.

parameters.name: input-encryption-public-key
parameters.description: Public key that must be used to encrypt the confidential information in the request

parameters.name: Accept-Language
parameters.description:
Language and country header.

The value is a 2-letter language code defined in ISO 639-1 alpha-2 followed by a 2-letter country code defined in ISO 3166-1 alpha-2, separated by a hyphen.

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: Account ID to be used in the query. If the account ID is provided in an identifiable form, it must be encrypted or tokenized.

parameters.name: first_level_code
parameters.description: Code for the highest category within the hierarchy used to identify the warnings and blocks to be included in the response. Examples of these categories include 'basic operations', 'management operations', and 'insolvency proceedings'.

parameters.name: second_level_code
parameters.description: Code for the second-highest category within the hierarchy used to identify the warnings and blocks to be included in the response. Examples of these categories include 'cash', 'securities portfolio', and 'tax collection and management'.

parameters.name: third_level_code
parameters.description: Code for the third-highest category within the hierarchy used to identify the warnings and blocks to be included in the response. Examples of these categories include 'cash deposits', 'purchase of stock exchange shares', and 'tax collection'.

parameters.name: status_code
parameters.description: Status code for the warnings and blocks to be included in the response

parameters.name: _offset
parameters.description:
Pagination identifier that is returned to the app by the API response when this uses the HATEOAS '_prev' or '_next' links.

If no offset is specified, the app can navigate from the last returned page to the next page, the previous page, or the first page.

parameters.name: _limit
parameters.description:
Maximum number of rows to be included on each page in the response.

Fewer rows can be returned if the query does not yield that many.

parameters.name: action_type_code
parameters.description: Code for the action type or response level of the warnings and blocks to be included in the response. Examples of actions include 'authorize', 'block', and 'hold pending authorization'.

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description:
Start date for the validity period of the warnings and blocks to be included in the response. The value uses the complete data format defined in ISO 8601: 'YYYY-MM-DD' Where:
  • YYYY: 4-digit year
  • MM: 2-digit month (for example, 01 = January)
  • DD: 2-digit day of the month (01 through 31)

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description:
End date for the validity period of the warnings and blocks to be included in the response. The value uses the complete data format defined in ISO 8601: 'YYYY-MM-DD' Where:
  • YYYY: 4-digit year
  • MM: 2-digit month (for example, 01 = January)
  • DD: 2-digit day of the month (01 through 31)

post.summary: Creates a warning or block for a specific account.
post.description:
Creates a warning or block for a specific account.
To send this request, you must provide an account ID in the request path.
If the account ID is provided in an identifiable form, it must be encrypted or tokenized.
Details of the warning or block to be created must be provided in the request body.
If at least one threshold has been defined, the warning or block activates only if the account 
balance crosses a threshold. If no thresholds have been defined, the warning or block activates 
immediately when created. The response includes the ID for the created warning or block.

parameters.name: input-encryption-public-key
parameters.description: Public key that must be used to encrypt the confidential information in the request

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: Account ID to be used in the query. If the account ID is provided in an identifiable form, it must be encrypted or tokenized.

post.summary: Converts a contract identifier to other formats.
post.description:
Converts a provided contract identifier to other formats. The available formats are internal, national, and international.

The contract identifier to be converted must be provided in the request body. Only one contract identifier can be converted.

The formats to which the contract identifier is to be converted can be provided in the '_fields' parameter. If no value is provided, the contract identifier is converted to all formats.

parameters.name: _fields
parameters.description:
Converted contract identifier formats to be included in the response.

The possible values are:

internal

national

international

Multiple values can be provided as a comma-separated list.

The default value is empty, in which case converted contract identifiers of all formats are returned.

requestBody.description: Data structure containing the request details for converting a contract identifier to other formats

post.description: Cancels a specific standing order. This operation is used to cancel a standing order for which a payment has already been made.

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: ID of the account to be used in the query.

parameters.name: standingOrderId
parameters.description: Standing order ID to be used in the query.

post.description: Cancels a specific bill payment. This operation is used to cancel a bill payment where there is an existing payment transaction.To send this request, you must provide a bill payment ID in the request path.The code for the current status of the bill payment to be cancelled must be provided in the request body.

requestBody.description: Data structure containing the request details for cancelling a bill payment

post.summary: Cancels an overdraft for an account.
post.description:
Cancels an overdraft for a specific account.

To send this request, you must provide an account ID and overdraft ID in the request path. If the account ID is provided in an identifiable form, it must be encrypted or tokenized.

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: Account ID to be used in the query. If the account ID is provided in an identifiable form, it must be encrypted or tokenized.

parameters.name: overdraftId
parameters.description: Overdraft ID to be canceled.

204.description:
No Content.

Response to a request to cancel an overdraft for an account.

get.summary: Retrieves a list of authorization holds for an account.
get.description:
Retrieves a list of authorization holds for a specific account.

Only authorization holds that are active for the specified account at the time of the request are included in the response. No cancelled authorization holds are retrieved.

To send this request, you must provide an account ID in the request path. If an account ID is provided in an identifiable form, it must be encrypted or tokenized.

The authorization holds included in the response can be filtered by activity period.

The response includes the period, amount, reason, and audit details for the retrieved authorization holds, as well as details of the associated legal process, if applicable.

parameters.name: Accept-Language
parameters.description:
Language and country header.

The value is a 2-letter language code defined in ISO 639-1 alpha-2 followed by a 2-letter country code defined in ISO 3166-1 alpha-2, separated by a hyphen.

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: Account ID to be used in the query. If the account ID is provided in an identifiable form, it must be encrypted or tokenized.

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description:
Start date of the activity period for the authorization holds to be included in the response. The value uses the complete data format defined in ISO 8601: 'YYYY-MM-DD' Where:
  • YYYY: 4-digit year
  • MM: 2-digit month (for example, 01 = January)
  • DD: 2-digit day of the month (01 through 31)

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description:
End date of the activity period for the authorization holds to be included in the response. The value uses the complete data format defined in ISO 8601: 'YYYY-MM-DD' Where:
  • YYYY: 4-digit year
  • MM: 2-digit month (for example, 01 = January)
  • DD: 2-digit day of the month (01 through 31)

post.summary: Creates an authorization for an account.
post.description:
Creates an authorization hold for a specific account. An authorization hold freezes an amount of the account balance in order to guarantee a subsequent payment for that amount. While the authorization hold is active, the available balance for the account is reduced by the authorization hold amount. To send this request, you must provide an account ID in the request path. If an account ID is provided in an identifiable form, it must be encrypted or tokenized. Details of the authorization hold to be created must be provided in the request body, including the following:
  • End date. If the authorization hold amount is not used by the end date, it is automatically unfrozen and added to the available balance for the account. The start date is the date of the request, as creation of authorization holds in the future is not allowed.
  • Details of the legal process associated with the hold, if applicable
  • Amount and currency. The currency must be that of the account.
The response includes the created authorization hold ID. This operation creates a single authorization hold. To create multiple authorization holds, multiple requests must be sent.

parameters.name: Accept-Language
parameters.description:
Language and country header.

The value is a 2-letter language code defined in ISO 639-1 alpha-2 followed by a 2-letter country code defined in ISO 3166-1 alpha-2, separated by a hyphen.

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: Account ID to be used in the query. If the account ID is provided in an identifiable form, it must be encrypted or tokenized.

201.description:
Created.

Response to a request to create an authorization hold for an account.

parameters.name: caseId
parameters.description: The case id

parameters.name: caseId
parameters.description: The case id

parameters.name: caseId
parameters.description: The case id

parameters.name: caseId
parameters.description: The case id

post.description: Validates if an account is valid according to the provided identifier

requestBody.description: Object containing the account identifier to validate

get.summary: Get accounts
get.description: Get accounts

parameters.name: participant_id
parameters.description: Participant ID associated with the operations for which accounts are to be included in the response or the account with which a phone number is to be associated

parameters.name: service_code
parameters.description: Service code that indicates the banking functionality's execution type for the accounts to be included in the response

parameters.name: type_code
parameters.description: List of type codes for the accounts to be included in the response

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Status of the accounts to be included in the response

parameters.name: _offset
parameters.description:
Pagination identifier that is returned to the app by the API response when this uses the HATEOAS '_prev' or '_next' links.

If no offset is specified, the app can navigate from the last returned page to the next page, the previous page, or the first page.

parameters.name: _limit
parameters.description:
Maximum number of rows to be included on each page in the response.

Fewer rows can be returned if the query does not yield that many.

parameters.name: _expand
parameters.description:
Whether information about account_balances is included for the retrieved account(s).

The possible value is:

AccountBalances = 'account_balances' array is returned.
If this parameter is omitted, the 'account_balances' array is not included for the retrieved account(s).

get.summary: Retrieves an account's details.
get.description:
Retrieves a specific account's details.

To send this request, you must provide an account ID in the request path. If an account ID is provided in an identifiable form, it must be encrypted or tokenized.

If the '_expand' parameter is provided and its value is 'Balances', the 'balances' array is included in the response for the retrieved account.

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description:
Account ID to be used in the query.

If an account ID is provided in an identifiable form, it must be encrypted or tokenized.

parameters.name: _expand
parameters.description:
Whether information about balances is included for the retrieved account(s).

The possible value is:

Balances = 'balances' array is returned.
If this parameter is omitted, the 'Balances' array is not included for the retrieved account(s).

parameters.name: input-encryption-public-key

get.summary: Retrieves a list of bill payments
get.description:
Retrieves a list of bill payments.

The bill payments included in the response can be filtered by the following criteria:

Status code and description
Customer ID
Mandate ID
Provider ID
Payment reference
End-to-end ID
Internal account ID
Next expected payment date or date and time
Period type code
Currency
Amount
Subtype code
The retrieved bill payments can also be sorted in ascending or descending order by multiple criteria provided in the '_sort' parameter.

parameters.name: status_code
parameters.description:
Status code for the bill payments to be included in the response.

Multiple values can be provided as a comma-separated list.

parameters.name: status_description
parameters.description: Status description of the bill payments to be included in the response.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Customer ID associated with the bill payments or service providers to be included in the response.

parameters.name: mandate_id
parameters.description: Mandate ID associated with the bill payments to be included in the response.

parameters.name: provider_id
parameters.description: Provider ID associated with the bill payments to be included in the response.

parameters.name: bill_payment_reference
parameters.description: Payment reference for the bill payments to be included in the response.

parameters.name: end_to_end_identification
parameters.description: End-to-end ID associated with the bill payments to be included in the response.

parameters.name: internal_identification
parameters.description: Internal account ID associated with the bill payments to be included in the response.

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description:
Start date for the period from which bill payments are to be included in the response or the bill payment summary, based on their next expected payment date.

The value uses the complete data format defined in ISO 8601:

'YYYY-MM-DD'

Where:

YYYY: 4-digit year
MM: 2-digit month (for example, 01 = January)
DD: 2-digit day of the month (01 through 31).

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description:
End date for the period from which bill payments are to be included in the response or the bill payment summary, based on their next expected payment date.

The value uses the complete data format defined in ISO 8601:

'YYYY-MM-DD'

Where:

YYYY: 4-digit year
MM: 2-digit month (for example, 01 = January)
DD: 2-digit day of the month (01 through 31).

parameters.name: start_date_time
parameters.description:
Start date and time for the period from which bill payments are to be included in the response, based on their next expected payment date.

The value uses the complete data format defined in ISO 8601:

'YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss.sssTZD'

Where:

YYYY: 4-digit year
MM: 2-digit month (for example, 01 = January)
DD: 2-digit day of the month (01 through 31)
hh: 2-digit hour of the day (00 through 23)
mm: 2-digit minute of the hour (00 through 59)
ss.sss: 5-digit seconds and milliseconds, separated by a point
TZD: Time zone indicator.

parameters.name: end_date_time
parameters.description:
End date and time for the period from which bill payments are to be included in the response, based on their next expected payment date.

The value uses the complete data format defined in ISO 8601:

'YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss.sssTZD'

Where:

YYYY: 4-digit year
MM: 2-digit month (for example, 01 = January)
DD: 2-digit day of the month (01 through 31)
hh: 2-digit hour of the day (00 through 23)
mm: 2-digit minute of the hour (00 through 59)
ss.sss: 5-digit seconds and milliseconds, separated by a point
TZD: Time zone indicator.

parameters.name: period_type_code
parameters.description:
Period type code for the bill payments to be included in the response.

Multiple values can be provided as a comma-separated list.

parameters.name: currency
parameters.description:
Currency for the bill payments to be included in the response.

The value is in the alpha-3 format defined in ISO 4217 (https://www.iso.org/iso-4217-currency-codes.html).

parameters.name: amount_from
parameters.description: Minimum amount for the bill payments to be included in the response.

parameters.name: amount_to
parameters.description: Maximum amount for the bill payments to be included in the response.

parameters.name: subtype_code
parameters.description: Subtype code for the bill payments to be included in the response.

parameters.name: subtype_code
parameters.description: Subtype code for the bill payments to be included in the response.

parameters.name: _limit
parameters.description:
Maximum number of rows to be included on each page in the response.

Fewer rows can be returned if the query does not yield that many.

parameters.name: _offset
parameters.description:
Pagination identifier that is returned to the app by the API response when this uses the HATEOAS '_prev' or '_next' links.

If no offset is specified, the app can navigate from the last returned page to the next page, the previous page, or the first page.

parameters.name: _sort
parameters.description:
Sorting order for the bill payments to be included in the response.

The possible values are, for example:

+executionDate = Ascending by execution date
-executionDate = Descending by execution date
+nextExpectedPaymentDate = Ascending by next expected payment date
-nextExpectedPaymentDate = Descending by next expected payment date
Multiple values can be provided as a comma-separated list.

get.summary: Retrieves a list of transactions for an account.
get.description:
Retrieves a list of transactions for a specific current or savings account.

To send this request, you must provide an account ID in the request path. If an account ID is provided in an identifiable form, it must be encrypted or tokenized.

The transactions included in the response can be filtered by operation date, amount, transaction type, type code, status, and description.

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: Account ID to be used in the query. If the account ID is provided in an identifiable form, it must be encrypted or tokenized.

parameters.name: _offset
parameters.description:
Pagination identifier that is returned to the app by the API response when this uses the HATEOAS '_prev' or '_next' links.

If no offset is specified, the app can navigate from the last returned page to the next page, the previous page, or the first page.

parameters.name: _limit
parameters.description:
Maximum number of rows to be included on each page in the response.

Fewer rows can be returned if the query does not yield that many.

parameters.name: _fields
parameters.description:
Information to be retrieved for each transaction to be included in the response.

The possible value is:

BasicData = Only the 'transactionId', 'description', 'processedDate', 'Amount.amount', and 'Amount.currency' fields are retrieved.
If this parameter is omitted, full transaction details are retrieved.

parameters.name: operation_date_from
parameters.description:
Start date for the period from which transactions are to be included in the response, based on their operation date.

The value uses the complete data format defined in ISO 8601:

'YYYY-MM-DD'

Where:

YYYY: 4-digit year
MM: 2-digit month (for example, 01 = January)
DD: 2-digit day of the month (01 through 31)
The value must not be a later date than the value of the 'operation_date_to' parameter.

parameters.name: operation_date_to
parameters.description:
End date for the period from which transactions are to be included in the response, based on their operation date.

The value uses the complete data format defined in ISO 8601:

'YYYY-MM-DD'

Where:

YYYY: 4-digit year
MM: 2-digit month (for example, 01 = January)
DD: 2-digit day of the month (01 through 31)
The value must not be an earlier date than the value of the 'operation_date_from' parameter.

parameters.name: amount_from
parameters.description:
Minimum amount for the transactions to be included in the response.

The value must not be greater than the value of the 'amount_to' parameter.

parameters.name: amount_to
parameters.description:
Maximum amount for the transactions to be included in the response.

The value must not be less than the value of the 'amount_from' parameter.

parameters.name: transaction_type
parameters.description:
Transaction type that indicates whether the balance for the transactions to be included in the response is in debit or credit.

The default value is empty, in which case transactions of all balances are included in the response.

Available values:

Credit = Transactions with credit balance.
Debit = Transactions with debit balance.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description:
Status of the transactions to be included in the response.

The possible values are:

Pending = Only provisional transactions are included.
Posted = Only consolidated transactions are included.
All = Both consolidated and provisional transactions are included.
The default value is 'All'.

parameters.name: type_code
parameters.description: Transaction type code for the transactions to be included in the response.

parameters.name: transaction_description
parameters.description: Description of the transactions to be included in the response.

get.summary: Retrieves a list of transactions for an account.
get.description:
Retrieves a list of transactions for a specific current or savings account.

To send this request, you must provide an account ID in the request path. If an account ID is provided in an identifiable form, it must be encrypted or tokenized.

The transactions included in the response can be filtered by operation date, amount, transaction type, type code, status, and description.

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: Account ID to be used in the query. If the account ID is provided in an identifiable form, it must be encrypted or tokenized.

parameters.name: _fields
parameters.description:
Information to be retrieved for each transaction to be included in the response.

The possible value is:

BasicData = Only the 'transactionId', 'description', 'processedDate', 'Amount.amount', and 'Amount.currency' fields are retrieved.
If this parameter is omitted, full transaction details are retrieved.

parameters.name: operation_date_from

The value uses the complete data format defined in ISO 8601:

'YYYY-MM-DD'

Where:

YYYY: 4-digit year
MM: 2-digit month (for example, 01 = January)
DD: 2-digit day of the month (01 through 31)
The value must not be a later date than the value of the 'operation_date_to' parameter.

parameters.name: operation_date_to
parameters.description:
End date for the period from which transactions are to be included in the response, based on their operation date.

The value uses the complete data format defined in ISO 8601:

'YYYY-MM-DD'

Where:

YYYY: 4-digit year
MM: 2-digit month (for example, 01 = January)
DD: 2-digit day of the month (01 through 31)
The value must not be an earlier date than the value of the 'operation_date_from' parameter.

parameters.name: amount_from
parameters.description:
Minimum amount for the transactions to be included in the response.

The value must not be greater than the value of the 'amount_to' parameter.

parameters.name: amount_to
parameters.description:
Maximum amount for the transactions to be included in the response.

The value must not be less than the value of the 'amount_from' parameter.

parameters.name: transaction_type
parameters.description:
Transaction type that indicates whether the balance for the transactions to be included in the response is in debit or credit.

The default value is empty, in which case transactions of all balances are included in the response.

Available values:

Credit = Transactions with credit balance.
Debit = Transactions with debit balance.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description:
Status of the transactions to be included in the response.

The possible values are:

Pending = Only provisional transactions are included.
Posted = Only consolidated transactions are included.
All = Both consolidated and provisional transactions are included.
The default value is 'All'.

parameters.name: type_code
parameters.description: Transaction type code for the transactions to be included in the response.

parameters.name: transaction_description

get.summary: Retrieves a list of overdrafts for an account
get.description:
Retrieves a list of overdrafts for a specific current or savings account.

To send this request, you must provide an account ID in the request path. If an account ID is provided in an identifiable form, it must be encrypted or tokenized.

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: Account ID to be used in the query. If the account ID is provided in an identifiable form, it must be encrypted or tokenized.

get.summary: Retrieves a list of authorization holds all for an account.
get.description: Retrieves a list of authorization holds all for a specific account.

parameters.name: Accept-Language
parameters.description:
Language and country header.

The value is a 2-letter language code defined in ISO 639-1 alpha-2 followed by a 2-letter country code defined in ISO 3166-1 alpha-2, separated by a hyphen.

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: Account ID to be used in the query. If the account ID is provided in an identifiable form, it must be encrypted or tokenized.

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description:
Start date of the activity period for the authorization holds to be included in the response. The value uses the complete data format defined in ISO 8601: 'YYYY-MM-DD' Where:
  • YYYY: 4-digit year
  • MM: 2-digit month (for example, 01 = January)
  • DD: 2-digit day of the month (01 through 31)

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description:
End date of the activity period for the authorization holds to be included in the response. The value uses the complete data format defined in ISO 8601: 'YYYY-MM-DD' Where:
  • YYYY: 4-digit year
  • MM: 2-digit month (for example, 01 = January)
  • DD: 2-digit day of the month (01 through 31)

get.summary: Get Account account_balances
get.description:
Retrieves a list of account_balances for a specific current or savings account.

To send this request, you must provide an account ID in the request path. If an account ID is provided in an identifiable form, it must be encrypted or tokenized.

You can specify the date for which account_balances are to be included in the response. If a date is not provided, the date of the request is used.

parameters.name: date
parameters.description:
Date for which account_balances are to be included in the response.

The value uses the complete data format defined in ISO 8601:

'YYYY-MM-DD'

Where:

YYYY: 4-digit year
MM: 2-digit month (for example, 01 = January)
DD: 2-digit day of the month (01 through 31)
The default value is the date of the request.

Example : 2025-04-01s

get.summary: Retrieve transactions for a specific placement
get.description: Fetches transactions associated with a specific placement based on various filters such as type code, credit/debit indicator, date range, and pagination.

get.summary: Retrieve placements for a specific deposit
get.description: Retrieve placements for a specific deposit based on various filters such as placement status, date range, and pagination.

parameters.name: depositId
parameters.description: Unique identifier for the deposit

parameters.name: placement_status
parameters.description:
Placement status for the placements to be included in the response.
           
           The possible values are:
           - A = Active (not expired)
           - P = Pending (pending funds)
           - C = Cancelled
           - V = Expired (with or without balance)
           - T = All (active, pending, cancelled, and expired)

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: Start date for the placements to be included in the response (format: yyyy-MM-dd)

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: End date for the placements to be included in the response (format: yyyy-MM-dd)

parameters.name: _limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of rows to be included on each page in the response.\n\nFewer rows can be returned if the query does not yield that many.

parameters.name: _offset
parameters.description: Pagination identifier that is returned to the app by the API response when this uses the HATEOAS '_prev' or '_next' links.\n\nIf no offset is specified, the app can navigate from the last returned page to the next page, the previous page, or the first page.

Code for the participation type that the participant for whom deposits are to be included in the response is

parameters.name: bank_id
parameters.description: Bank ID associated with the deposits to be included in the response

parameters.name: placement_status
parameters.description:
Placement status for the deposits to be included in the response.

    The possible values are:
    - A = Active (not expired)
    - P = Pending (pending funds)
    - C = Cancelled
    - V = Expired (with or without balance)
    - T = All (active, pending, cancelled, and expired)

parameters.name: _limit
parameters.description:
Maximum number of rows to be included on each page in the response.

Fewer rows can be returned if the query does not yield that many.

parameters.name: _offset
parameters.description:
Pagination identifier that is returned to the app by the API response when this uses the HATEOAS '_prev' or '_next' links.

If no offset is specified, the app can navigate from the last returned page to the next page, the previous page, or the first page.

settlementReasonCode.description: Code for the reason to execute a settlement

additionalReasonSettlement.description: Additional information about the settlement execution reason

CenterCancellation.description: Data structure containing information about a bank centre

StatusInfoCancellation.description: Data structure containing information about an item's status

currency.description:
Currency code.

The value is in the alpha-3 format defined in ISO 4217 (https://www.iso.org/iso-4217-currency-codes.html).

amount.description:
Amount.

The value uses the data format defined in ISO 20022 and has a maximum of 18 digits, of which 5 can be decimals, separated by a point.

PostCancellationSettlement.description: Response containing amount and balance result

Incident.description: Data structure containing information about an incident

isBlock.description: Whether the incident is a block

IncidentListResponse.description: Data structure containing information about an account

CancelAccountRequest.description: Data structure containing information about an account

cancellationTypeCode.description: Code for the account cancellation type

settlementAccountId.description: ID for the account that is debited or credited by the settlement amount

CancellationReason.description: Data structure containing information about the reason for a cancellation request

Till.description: Data structure containing information about a cash till, including its attributes and cash position. A till can be, for example, the main till of a centre or vault, a counter till, or an ATM till.

amount.description:
Amount.

The value uses the data format defined in ISO 20022 and has a maximum of 18 digits, of which 5 can be decimals, separated by a point.

currency.description:
Currency code.

The value is in the alpha-3 format defined in ISO 4217 (https://www.iso.org/iso-4217-currency-codes.html).

Center.description: Data structure containing information about a bank centre

reasonTypeCode.description: Code for the warning or block type

additionalInformation.description: Additional information about the warning or block, provided as free text by the user who created it
additionalInformation.example: Blocked because the customer has several defaults

actionTypeCode.description: Code for the action type that a warning or a block can perform. Examples of actions include 'authorize', 'block', and 'hold pending authorization'.

actionTypeDescription.description: Description of the action type that a warning or a block can perform. Examples of actions include 'authorize', 'block', and 'hold pending authorization'.

availableBalance.description:
Data structure containing the thresholds for the available balance of the account to activate the warning or block.

The defined amount must be such that, after the operation, the account's available balance is less than or equal to the amount defined in the 'toAmount' data structure.

The currency must be the currency of the account's available balance.

principalBalance.description:
Data structure containing the thresholds for the main balance of the account to activate the warning or block.

The defined amount must be such that, after the operation, the account's main balance is less than or equal to the amount defined in the 'toAmount' data structure.

The currency must be the currency of the account's main balance.

operationAmount.description: Data structure containing information about the operation amount of the warning or block

reason.example: The amount exceeds the account limit.

statusInfo.description: The final status of the payment

AccountHoldCreatePeriod.description: Data structure containing information about a period

endDate.description:
Date when the period ends. The value uses the complete data format defined in ISO 8601: 'YYYY-MM-DD' Where:
  • YYYY: 4-digit year
  • MM: 2-digit month (for example, 01 = January)
  • DD: 2-digit day of the month (01 through 31)

endDateTime.description:
Date and time when the period ends. The value uses the complete data format defined in ISO 8601: 'YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss.sssTZD' Where:
  • YYYY: 4-digit year
  • MM: 2-digit month (for example, 01 = January)
  • DD: 2-digit day of the month (01 through 31)
  • hh: 2-digit hour of the day (00 through 23)
  • mm: 2-digit minute of the hour (00 through 59)
  • ss.sss: 5-digit seconds and milliseconds, separated by a point
  • TZD: Time zone indicator

AccountHoldCreateRequest.description: Data structure containing information about an operation hold for an account

isManageableByOthers.description: Whether the hold owner allows other users to manage the hold. For example, if the value is set to false, branch employees are prevented from cancelling holds generated by the payment application.

withholdingAboveAvailableIndicator.description: Whether a withholding can be applied above the account available balance

overdraftInAvailableIndicator.description: Whether the authorized overdraft is accounted for in the available account  balance

LegalProcess.description: Data structure containing information about a legal process

courtUnitCode.description: Code for the court jurisdiction unit

courtUnitDescription.description: Description of the court jurisdiction unit

LegalProcessPeriod.description: Data structure containing information about a period

AccountHoldCreateResponse.description: Data structure containing information about an operation hold

AccountHoldsResponse.description: Data structure containing the response information for retrieving a list of authorization holds for an account

_links.description: PageLinks to the next and previous pages with element count

blocksResult.description: Result of the delete blocks operation

Account.description: Data structure containing information about an account

specialSituationCode.description: Code for the account's special status

openingDate.description:
Date on which the account and related basic services are operational for the account owner.

The value uses the complete data format defined in ISO 8601:

'YYYY-MM-DD'

Where:

YYYY: 4-digit year
MM: 2-digit month (for example, 01 = January)
DD: 2-digit day of the month (01 through 31)

closingDate.description:
Date on which the account and related services cease to be operational for the account owner.

The value uses the complete data format defined in ISO 8601:

'YYYY-MM-DD'

Where:

YYYY: 4-digit year
MM: 2-digit month (for example, 01 = January)
DD: 2-digit day of the month (01 through 31)

balances.description: Array of account_balances. This array is included only if the '_expand' parameter is provided and its value is 'AccountBalances'.

AccountBalances.description: Data structure containing a numerical representation of the net increases and decreases in an account at a specific point in time

creditDebitIndicator.description: Whether the balance is in debit or credit

lastUpdateDate.description:
Date when the balance was last updated.

The value uses the complete data format defined in ISO 8601:

'YYYY-MM-DD'

Where:

YYYY: 4-digit year
MM: 2-digit month (for example, 01 = January)
DD: 2-digit day of the month (01 through 31)

AccountIdentification.description: Data structure containing information related to account identification

internalIdentification.description: Internal account ID, such as the account number in Partenón or in Altair

nationalIdentification.description:
National bank account number.

The format is dependent on the country in which the account is held.

In Spain, the CCC format is: 'EEEEBBBBCCAAAAAAAAAA'

Where:

E = Entity ID
B = Branch ID
C = Control digit
A = Account number
In the UK, the sort code format is: 'SSSSSSAAAAAAAA'

Where:

S = National sort code (6 digits). See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Industry_Sorting_Code_Directory.
A = Account number (7 or 8 digits)
In Portugal, the BCP format is: 'EEEEBBBBAAAAAAAAAAACC'

Where:

E = Entity ID
B = Branch ID
A = Account number
C = Control digit
In Germany, the BLZ format is: 'AAAAAAAAAABBBBBBBB'

Where:

A = Account number (maximum of 10 digits)
B = BLZ sort code (8 digits). See https://www.bundesbank.de/Navigation/EN/Tasks/Payment_systems/Services/Bank_sort_codes/bank_sort_codes.html/.
In the USA, the ABA format is: 'RRRRRRRRRAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA'

Where:

R = Routing number reference. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Routing_transit_number#Routing_number_format.
A = Account number (maximum of 17 digits)

reasonTypeDescription.description: Description of the warning or block type

additionalInformation.description: Additional information about the warning or block, provided as free text by the user who created it
additionalInformation.example: Blocked because the customer has several defaults

actionTypeCode.description: Code for the action type that a warning or a block can perform. Examples of actions include 'authorize', 'block', and 'hold pending authorization'.

actionTypeDescription.description: Description of the action type that a warning or a block can perform. Examples of actions include 'authorize', 'block', and 'hold pending authorization'.

availableBalance.description:
Data structure containing the thresholds for the available balance of the account to activate the warning or block.

The defined amount must be such that, after the operation, the account's available balance is less than or equal to the amount defined in the 'toAmount' data structure.

The currency must be the currency of the account's available balance.

principalBalance.description:

  • YYYY: 4-digit year

WarningBlockLevels.description: Data structure containing the possible levels for an account warning or block

firstLevelCode.description: Code for the highest category within the hierarchy used to identify a warning or a block. Examples of these categories include 'basic operations', 'management operations', and 'insolvency proceedings'.

firstLevelDescription.description: Description of the highest category within the hierarchy used to identify a warning or a block. Examples of these categories include 'basic operations', 'management operations', and 'insolvency proceedings'.

secondLevelCode.description: Code for the second highest category within the hierarchy used to identify a warning or a block. Examples of these categories include 'cash', 'securities portfolio', and 'tax collection and management'.

secondLevelDescription.description: Description of the second highest category within the hierarchy used to identify a warning or a block. Examples of these categories include 'cash', 'securities portfolio', and 'tax collection and management'.

thirdLevelCode.description: Code for the third highest category within the hierarchy used to identify a warning or a block. Examples of these categories include 'cash deposits', 'purchase of stock exchange shares', and 'tax collection'.

thirdLevelDescription.description: Description of the third highest category within the hierarchy used to identify a warning or a block. Examples of these categories include 'cash deposits', 'purchase of stock exchange shares', and 'tax collection'.

statusDate.description:
Date when the status was established. The value uses the complete data format defined in ISO 8601:'YYYY-MM-DD' Where:
  • YYYY: 4-digit year
  • MM: 2-digit month (for example, 01 = January)
  • DD: 2-digit day of the month (01 through 31)

AccountOverdraft.description:
Data structure containing information about an account's overdraft.

The overdraft amount is taken into account in the available balance calculation, meaning that the overdraft increases the available amount of the account.

creationDate.description:
Date when the overdraft was created. The value uses the complete data format defined in ISO 8601: 'YYYY-MM-DD' Where:
  • YYYY: 4-digit year
  • MM: 2-digit month (for example, 01 = January)
  • DD: 2-digit day of the month (01 through 31)

expirationDate.description:
Date when the overdraft expires. The value uses the complete data format defined in ISO 8601: 'YYYY-MM-DD' Where:
  • YYYY: 4-digit year
  • MM: 2-digit month (for example, 01 = January)
  • DD: 2-digit day of the month (01 through 31)

regularizationDate.description:
Date when the overdraft is regularized. The value uses the complete data format defined in ISO 8601: 'YYYY-MM-DD' Where:
  • YYYY: 4-digit year
  • MM: 2-digit month (for example, 01 = January)
  • DD: 2-digit day of the month (01 through 31)

reasonCode.description: Code for the reason why the overdraft was assigned

reasonDescription.description: Description of the reason why the overdraft was assigned

AccountOverdraftResponse.description: Data structure containing the response information for retrieving a list of overdrafts for an account

OverdraftStatusInfo.description: Data structure containing information about an item's status

Hold.description: Data structure containing information about an operation hold for an account

HoldAudit.description: Data structure containing information for auditing purposes

creationUser.description: ID of the user who created the data

creationDateTime.description:
Date and time when the data was created. The value uses the complete data format defined in ISO 8601: 'YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss.sssTZD' Where:
  • YYYY: 4-digit year
  • MM: 2-digit month (for example, 01 = January)
  • DD: 2-digit day of the month (01 through 31)
  • hh: 2-digit hour of the day (00 through 23)
  • mm: 2-digit minute of the hour (00 through 59)
  • ss.sss: 5-digit seconds and milliseconds, separated by a point
  • TZD: Time zone indicator

deletionUser.description: ID of the user who deleted the data

deletionDateTime.description:
Date and time when the data was deleted. The value uses the complete data format defined in ISO 8601: 'YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss.sssTZD' Where:
  • YYYY: 4-digit year
  • MM: 2-digit month (for example, 01 = January)
  • DD: 2-digit day of the month (01 through 31)
  • hh: 2-digit hour of the day (00 through 23)
  • mm: 2-digit minute of the hour (00 through 59)
  • ss.sss: 5-digit seconds and milliseconds, separated by a point
  • TZD: Time zone indicator

HoldDetail.description: Data structure containing information about an operation hold for an account

isManageableByOthers.description: Whether the hold owner allows other users to manage the hold. For example, if the value is set to false, branch employees are prevented from cancelling holds generated by the payment application.

withholdingAboveAvailableIndicator.description: Whether a withholding can be applied above the account available balance

overdraftInAvailableIndicator.description: Whether the authorized overdraft is accounted for in the available account  balance

Period.description: Data structure containing information about a period

startDateTime.description:
Date and time when the period starts. The value uses the complete data format defined in ISO 8601: 'YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss.sssTZD' Where:
  • YYYY: 4-digit year
  • MM: 2-digit month (for example, 01 = January)
  • DD: 2-digit day of the month (01 through 31)
  • hh: 2-digit hour of the day (00 through 23)
  • mm: 2-digit minute of the hour (00 through 59)
  • ss.sss: 5-digit seconds and milliseconds, separated by a point
  • TZD: Time zone indicator

endDateTime.description:
Date and time when the period ends. The value uses the complete data format defined in ISO 8601: 'YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss.sssTZD' Where:
  • YYYY: 4-digit year
  • MM: 2-digit month (for example, 01 = January)
  • DD: 2-digit day of the month (01 through 31)
  • hh: 2-digit hour of the day (00 through 23)
  • mm: 2-digit minute of the hour (00 through 59)
  • ss.sss: 5-digit seconds and milliseconds, separated by a point
  • TZD: Time zone indicator

TermDepositsDepositIdentification.description: Data structure containing identification information for a deposit

internalIdentification.description: Internal deposit ID, such as the deposit number in internal systems (Partenón, Altair, or client backend software)

nationalIdentification.description: National bank deposit number. This number is confidential and its last 4 digits must be obfuscated

maturityDate.description: Date when the placement is due

creationDate.description:
Date when the data was created.

The value uses the complete data format defined in ISO 8601:

'YYYY-MM-DD'

Where:

YYYY: 4-digit year
MM: 2-digit month (for example, 01 = January)
DD: 2-digit day of the month (01 through 31)
